body.text: Crypto News Bot — Daily Digest
USDC now commands ~70% of stablecoin transaction volume vs ~25% for USDT, as MiCA's July 1 deadline pushes EU exchanges to delist non-compliant USDT.
Stablecoin News
GENIUS Act capital/reserve rules due July 18
Six federal agencies are finalizing GENIUS Act stablecoin rules, with the OCC setting a $5M capital floor and the FDIC confirming no deposit insurance applies to token holders.
EU exchanges restrict USDT after MiCA deadline
Binance, Kraken, Coinbase and others have delisted or restricted USDT for EU users since MiCA's July 1 transitional deadline ended; Revolut will drop USDT for EEA/Swiss users by Aug 31.
USDC overtakes USDT in transaction volume
Circle's USDC drove ~70% of adjusted stablecoin transaction volume in H1 2026 versus ~25% for Tether's USDT, per JPMorgan and industry data, as stablecoin volume hits record $1.79T.
UK finalizes systemic stablecoin oversight plan
The Bank of England and FCA are building a two-tier UK regime: FCA oversight for all qualifying stablecoins, plus joint supervision for issuers deemed systemic; consultation closes Sept 22.
Hacking & Security Incidents
"Hackers' Day": three DeFi exploits, $35.55M lost
AFX Trade (Arbitrum perps DEX) lost $24.15M, VerusCoin's Ethereum bridge lost $7.54M, and B² Network lost $3.86M in separate attacks within hours on July 23.
Verus Ethereum bridge drained again via old exploit path
Attackers reused the same import-path vulnerability weaponized in May to steal $7.54M from the Verus Ethereum Bridge, its second breach in just over two months.
B² Network loses $3.86M to upgrade-authority hijack
An attacker seized upgrade authority over B² Network's staking contract, draining roughly $3.86M in the third "Hackers' Day" incident.
Ostium hit for up to $24M via oracle key compromise
Arbitrum-based perpetuals exchange Ostium suffered an oracle manipulation exploit after an attacker compromised a price-oracle private key, pushing fake future-dated price signs onchain.
